title: Upstart Announces First Quarter 2026 Results
text: Upstart achieved significant growth in loan originations during fiscal Q1 2026. Total originations reached $3.4 billion, representing 61% year-over-year growth and 8% sequential increase. More than 425,000 loans originated during the quarter. Revenue came to approximately $308 million, up 44% annually and 4% sequentially. Fee revenue climbed to roughly $277 million, increasing 49% year-over-year. Core Personal Loans remained flat sequentially against historical Q1 declines of roughly 10%, indicating better-than-typical seasonal performance. Auto Products originations surged over 300% year-over-year and 30% sequentially, with retail auto originations jumping roughly 13x annually. Home Products originations increased 250% year-over-year and 16% sequentially, with over 25% of home loans fully automated and an average six-day closing timeline. Despite strong origination growth, Q1 profitability declined marginally due to front-loaded expenses and product mix shifts. Management reaffirmed full-year guidance of $1.4 billion in revenue and $294 million in adjusted EBITDA.
